title: Apartments for Rent in Shaker Heights, OH
metaTitle: Apartments for Rent in Shaker Heights, OH
metaDescription: Apartments for rent in Shaker Heights, OH near the RTA Blue/Green rail. See rent ranges and Section 8 voucher basics. Contact our Cleveland leasing team today.
primaryKeyword: apartments for rent Shaker Heights ohio
answerBox: Shaker Heights, Ohio is an East Side Cuyahoga County suburb served directly by the RTA's Blue and Green light-rail lines, bordering Cleveland's Buckeye-Shaker neighborhood (ZIP 44120), where Rent Finder Cleveland works with voucher-friendly rental homes. Greater Cleveland apartments generally rent from about $1,060 to $1,350 a month, per Zumper's July 2026 report.

sections.h2: Renting an apartment in Shaker Heights, Ohio
body: Shaker Heights lies directly southeast of Cleveland, bordering the city's Buckeye-Shaker neighborhood along Shaker Boulevard and the RTA Blue/Green light-rail corridor. Housing here ranges from early-20th-century garden apartment buildings near Shaker Square to newer development farther out toward the Van Aken District and Green Road, giving renters a wide spread of building ages and unit sizes within a single suburb.

We don't currently work with rental units inside Shaker Heights, but immediately across the line in Buckeye-Shaker (**ZIP 44120**), Rent Finder Cleveland works with voucher-friendly rental homes along the same Shaker Boulevard corridor. [See our Buckeye-Shaker Square listings](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/houses-for-rent-buckeye-shaker-square-cleveland/), or tell us what you're looking for in Shaker Heights and we'll follow up.

sections.h2: What kind of apartments are available in Shaker Heights?
body: Shaker Heights was planned in the early 20th century around its rail lines, so much of its apartment stock sits within walking distance of the Blue and Green Line stations — courtyard buildings and mid-rise apartments near Shaker Square and Fairhill Road, with additional inventory farther out near Van Aken and Warrensville Center Road. Renters comparing Shaker Heights to the Cleveland side of Shaker Boulevard should expect a similar range of building ages, since Buckeye-Shaker and Shaker Heights grew up together along the same light-rail corridor.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent in Shaker Heights?
body: Rent Finder Cleveland's own managed homes across Greater Cleveland typically range from about $700 to $1,800 a month, most commonly around $1,000 — well below the averages reported for larger, professionally managed apartment buildings across the metro. Shaker Heights, with its mix of prewar apartment buildings near Shaker Square and newer construction, tends to price above the broader Cleveland range shown below.

Shaker Heights is one of the few Cuyahoga County suburbs with its own rail stations: the RTA's Blue Line (Van Aken branch) and Green Line (Green Road branch) both run from downtown Cleveland's Tower City through Shaker Heights along Shaker Boulevard. Local [RTA bus routes](https://www.riderta.com/routes) connect to University Circle and to the Buckeye-Shaker area of Cleveland just across the line.

items: **Transit:** RTA Blue Line (Van Aken) and Green Line (Green Rd) light rail to Tower City downtown
**Districts:** Shaker Square and the Van Aken District

body: Housing Choice Vouchers are administered countywide through CMHA, and every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with — including our rentals just across the line in Buckeye-Shaker — accepts vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, so check directly with any Shaker Heights landlord about their voucher policy; see our [guide to Cleveland-area source-of-income rules](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/source-of-income-discrimination-cleveland-ohio/) for which local municipalities currently ban refusing a voucher outright.

sections.h2: Finding a rental near Shaker Heights
body: Our closest available homes to Shaker Heights are typically in the adjoining Buckeye-Shaker area of Cleveland, along the same Blue/Green rail corridor. [Tell our team what you need](/p/book-showing), or read our comparison of [renting in Cleveland versus its suburbs](/cleveland-rentals/guides/cleveland-vs-suburbs-renting/) before deciding where to focus your search.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with apartments in Shaker Heights?
faq.a: Not directly. Our voucher-friendly rental homes are concentrated in the City of Cleveland, with availability just across the line from Shaker Heights in the Buckeye-Shaker neighborhood (ZIP 44120). Tell our team what you're looking for in Shaker Heights and we'll point you to current nearby availability.

faq.q: How much is rent in Shaker Heights?
faq.a: Our own managed homes across Greater Cleveland typically range from about $700 to $1,800 a month, most commonly around $1,000. Shaker Heights, with prewar apartment buildings near Shaker Square and newer construction, tends to price above the broader Zumper and RentCafe metro figures for Greater Cleveland (both July 2026).

faq.q: Does Shaker Heights have its own RTA rail station?
faq.a: Yes. Shaker Heights is served directly by the RTA's Blue Line (Van Aken branch) and Green Line (Green Road branch), both running along Shaker Boulevard to downtown Cleveland's Tower City — one of the only Cuyahoga County suburbs with its own light-rail stops.

faq.q: Does Shaker Heights accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Housing Choice Vouchers are administered countywide by CMHA, so a voucher can potentially be used in Shaker Heights if a landlord participates. Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, so confirm voucher policy directly with any Shaker Heights landlord before applying.

faq.q: Is Shaker Heights part of the City of Cleveland?
faq.a: No. Shaker Heights is an independent Cuyahoga County suburb, not a Cleveland neighborhood. It borders the city directly along Shaker Boulevard, sharing a line with Cleveland's Buckeye-Shaker area and the same RTA light-rail corridor that continues into Shaker Square.
